chooseResponse.q: Are you attending the workshop tomorrow?

conversation.transcript:
Student: Have you signed up for the environmental science workshop?
Advisor: Yes, I have. It seems really interesting! Have you?
Student: Not yet. What topics will they cover?
Advisor: They’ll discuss climate change and sustainable practices. I think it’s very relevant.

questions.q: What is the main topic of the workshop?
questions.explain: 工作坊的主要主題是氣候變遷。

questions.q: What did the student say about signing up?

announcement.transcript: Attention all students: The campus library will be closed for maintenance this Saturday. Please plan your study schedule accordingly. We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. Thank you for your understanding.

questions.q: When will the library be closed?
questions.explain: 圖書館將在本週六關閉。
圖書館關閉是因為維護。

talk.transcript: Today, I want to discuss the concept of biodiversity. Biodiversity refers to the variety of life forms on Earth, including the different species of plants, animals, and microorganisms, as well as the ecosystems they form. High biodiversity is essential for ecosystem resilience, allowing ecosystems to recover from disturbances. Additionally, it provides numerous benefits such as food, medicine, and ecosystem services like pollination and water purification. Loss of biodiversity can lead to ecosystem collapse and reduced resources for humans and wildlife alike. Protecting biodiversity is crucial for maintaining the balance of our planet.

questions.explain: 生物多樣性是指生命形式的多樣性。
高生物多樣性對維持生態系統的韌性至關重要。

questions.q: What can loss of biodiversity lead to?
questions.explain: 生物多樣性的喪失可能導致生態系統崩潰。

questions.q: What is one benefit of biodiversity mentioned?
questions.explain: 提到的生物多樣性的一個好處是食物來源。
